authors_text: Avinash Ummadisingu,Kuniyuki Takahashi,Naoki Fukaya
title: Cluttered Food Grasping with Adaptive Fingers and Synthetic-Data Trained Object Detection
title_zh: 使用自适应手指和合成数据训练的物体检测来抓取杂乱的食物
comment: 8 pages. Accepted at ICRA2022. An accompanying video is available at https://youtu.be/H0Mxo_xSxzw
comments: 8 pages. Accepted at ICRA2022. An accompanying video is available at https://youtu.be/H0Mxo_xSxzw
abstract: The food packaging industry handles an immense variety of food products with wide-ranging shapes and sizes, even within one kind of food. Menus are also diverse and change frequently, making automation of pick-and-place difficult. A popular approach to bin-picking is to first identify each piece of food in the tray by using an instance segmentation method. However, human annotations to train these methods are unreliable and error-prone since foods are packed close together with unclear boundaries and visual similarity making separation of pieces difficult. To address this problem, we propose a method that trains purely on synthetic data and successfully transfers to the real world using sim2real methods by creating datasets of filled food trays using high-quality 3d models of real pieces of food for the training instance segmentation models. Another concern is that foods are easily damaged during grasping. We address this by introducing two additional methods -- a novel adaptive finger mechanism to passively retract when a collision occurs, and a method to filter grasps that are likely to cause damage to neighbouring pieces of food during a grasp. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on several kinds of real foods.
summary: The food packaging industry handles an immense variety of food products with wide-ranging shapes and sizes, even within one kind of food. Menus are also diverse and change frequently, making automation of pick-and-place difficult. A popular approach to bin-picking is to first identify each piece of food in the tray by using an instance segmentation method. However, human annotations to train these methods are unreliable and error-prone since foods are packed close together with unclear boundaries and visual similarity making separation of pieces difficult. To address this problem, we propose a method that trains purely on synthetic data and successfully transfers to the real world using sim2real methods by creating datasets of filled food trays using high-quality 3d models of real pieces of food for the training instance segmentation models. Another concern is that foods are easily damaged during grasping. We address this by introducing two additional methods -- a novel adaptive finger mechanism to passively retract when a collision occurs, and a method to filter grasps that are likely to cause damage to neighbouring pieces of food during a grasp. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on several kinds of real foods.
abstract_zh: 食品包装行业处理的食品种类繁多，形状和尺寸各异，甚至是一种食品。菜单也多种多样并且经常变化，使得拾放的自动化变得困难。一种流行的分箱方法是首先使用实例分割方法来识别托盘中的每件食物。然而，训练这些方法的人工注释不可靠且容易出错，因为食物紧密地包装在一起，边界不清晰且视觉相似，使得食物的分离变得困难。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种方法，该方法纯粹基于合成数据进行训练，并使用 sim2real 方法成功地转移到现实世界，方法是使用真实食物的高质量 3D 模型创建填充食物托盘的数据集，用于训练实例分割模型。另一个问题是食物在抓握过程中很容易损坏。我们通过引入两种额外的方法来解决这个问题——一种新颖的自适应手指机制，用于在发生碰撞时被动缩回，以及一种过滤抓取过程中可能对相邻食物造成损坏的抓取方法。我们证明了所提出的方法对几种真实食物的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合自适应手指和基于合成数据训练的对象检测方法，解决杂乱食物抓取中的检测与损伤问题。
AI.motivation: 食品包装行业面临食物形状多样、人工标注分割困难及抓取易损问题，传统方法难以适应频繁变化的菜单和紧密包装场景。
AI.method: 使用高精度3D模型生成合成数据训练实例分割模型，并引入自适应手指碰撞回缩机制及抓取损伤过滤算法。
AI.result: 方法在多种真实食物上成功实现有效抓取，验证了合成数据迁移学习与损伤规避策略的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为食品分拣自动化提供了可靠方案，通过合成数据训练与机械设计创新解决了杂乱场景下的检测与抓取难题。

search_text: Cluttered Food Grasping with Adaptive Fingers and Synthetic-Data Trained Object Detection 使用自适应手指和合成数据训练的物体检测来抓取杂乱的食物 The food packaging industry handles an immense variety of food products with wide-ranging shapes and sizes, even within one kind of food. Menus are also diverse and change frequently, making automation of pick-and-place difficult. A popular approach to bin-picking is to first identify each piece of food in the tray by using an instance segmentation method. However, human annotations to train these methods are unreliable and error-prone since foods are packed close together with unclear boundaries and visual similarity making separation of pieces difficult. To address this problem, we propose a method that trains purely on synthetic data and successfully transfers to the real world using sim2real methods by creating datasets of filled food trays using high-quality 3d models of real pieces of food for the training instance segmentation models. Another concern is that foods are easily damaged during grasping. We address this by introducing two additional methods -- a novel adaptive finger mechanism to passively retract when a collision occurs, and a method to filter grasps that are likely to cause damage to neighbouring pieces of food during a grasp. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on several kinds of real foods. 食品包装行业处理的食品种类繁多，形状和尺寸各异，甚至是一种食品。菜单也多种多样并且经常变化，使得拾放的自动化变得困难。一种流行的分箱方法是首先使用实例分割方法来识别托盘中的每件食物。然而，训练这些方法的人工注释不可靠且容易出错，因为食物紧密地包装在一起，边界不清晰且视觉相似，使得食物的分离变得困难。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种方法，该方法纯粹基于合成数据进行训练，并使用 sim2real 方法成功地转移到现实世界，方法是使用真实食物的高质量 3D 模型创建填充食物托盘的数据集，用于训练实例分割模型。另一个问题是食物在抓握过程中很容易损坏。我们通过引入两种额外的方法来解决这个问题——一种新颖的自适应手指机制，用于在发生碰撞时被动缩回，以及一种过滤抓取过程中可能对相邻食物造成损坏的抓取方法。我们证明了所提出的方法对几种真实食物的有效性。 本文提出一种结合自适应手指和基于合成数据训练的对象检测方法，解决杂乱食物抓取中的检测与损伤问题。 Avinash Ummadisingu,Kuniyuki Takahashi,Naoki Fukaya cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Yang Jiao,Zequn Jie,Jingjing Chen,Lin Ma,Yu-Gang Jiang
title: Suspected Object Matters: Rethinking Model's Prediction for One-stage Visual Grounding
title_zh: 可疑物体问题：重新思考模型对一阶段视觉接地的预测
abstract: Recently, one-stage visual grounders attract high attention due to their comparable accuracy but significantly higher efficiency than two-stage grounders. However, inter-object relation modeling has not been well studied for one-stage grounders. Inter-object relationship modeling, though important, is not necessarily performed among all objects, as only part of them are related to the text query and may confuse the model. We call these objects suspected objects. However, exploring their relationships in the one-stage paradigm is non-trivial because: First, no object proposals are available as the basis on which to select suspected objects and perform relationship modeling. Second, suspected objects are more confusing than others, as they may share similar semantics, be entangled with certain relationships, etc, and thereby more easily mislead the model prediction. Toward this end, we propose a Suspected Object Transformation mechanism (SOT), which can be seamlessly integrated into existing CNN and Transformer-based one-stage visual grounders to encourage the target object selection among the suspected ones. Suspected objects are dynamically discovered from a learned activation map adapted to the model current discrimination ability during training. Afterward, on top of suspected objects, a Keyword-Aware Discrimination module (KAD) and an Exploration by Random Connection strategy (ERC) are concurrently proposed to help the model rethink its initial prediction. On the one hand, KAD leverages keywords contributing high to suspected object discrimination. On the other hand, ERC allows the model to seek the correct object instead of being trapped in a situation that always exploits the current false prediction. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.
summary: Recently, one-stage visual grounders attract high attention due to their comparable accuracy but significantly higher efficiency than two-stage grounders. However, inter-object relation modeling has not been well studied for one-stage grounders. Inter-object relationship modeling, though important, is not necessarily performed among all objects, as only part of them are related to the text query and may confuse the model. We call these objects suspected objects. However, exploring their relationships in the one-stage paradigm is non-trivial because: First, no object proposals are available as the basis on which to select suspected objects and perform relationship modeling. Second, suspected objects are more confusing than others, as they may share similar semantics, be entangled with certain relationships, etc, and thereby more easily mislead the model prediction. Toward this end, we propose a Suspected Object Transformation mechanism (SOT), which can be seamlessly integrated into existing CNN and Transformer-based one-stage visual grounders to encourage the target object selection among the suspected ones. Suspected objects are dynamically discovered from a learned activation map adapted to the model current discrimination ability during training. Afterward, on top of suspected objects, a Keyword-Aware Discrimination module (KAD) and an Exploration by Random Connection strategy (ERC) are concurrently proposed to help the model rethink its initial prediction. On the one hand, KAD leverages keywords contributing high to suspected object discrimination. On the other hand, ERC allows the model to seek the correct object instead of being trapped in a situation that always exploits the current false prediction. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.
abstract_zh: 最近，一级视觉接地器由于其精度相当但比两级接地器显着更高的效率而引起了高度关注。然而，对于单阶段基础的对象间关系建模尚未得到很好的研究。对象间关系建模虽然很重要，但不一定在所有对象之间执行，因为只有部分对象与文本查询相关，并且可能会混淆模型。我们称这些物体为可疑物体。然而，在单阶段范式中探索它们的关系并非易事，因为：首先，没有对象建议可用作选择可疑对象和执行关系建模的基础。其次，可疑对象比其他对象更容易混淆，因为它们可能具有相似的语义，与某些关系纠缠在一起等，从而更容易误导模型预测。为此，我们提出了一种可疑对象转换机制（SOT），它可以无缝集成到现有的 CNN 和基于 Transformer 的单阶段视觉基础中，以鼓励在可疑对象中选择目标对象。在训练期间，从适应模型当前辨别能力的学习激活图动态发现可疑对象。随后，在可疑对象之上，同时提出了关键字感知判别模块（KAD）和随机连接探索策略（ERC），以帮助模型重新思考其初始预测。一方面，KAD 利用对可疑对象歧视有较高贡献的关键词。另一方面，ERC 允许模型寻找正确的对象，而不是陷入总是利用当前错误预测的情况。大量的实验证明了我们提出的方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对单阶段视觉定位模型的改进方法，通过处理可能干扰模型预测的‘疑似对象’来提升性能。
AI.motivation: 单阶段视觉定位模型虽高效，但未充分研究对象间关系建模。部分与文本无关的‘疑似对象’可能混淆模型，导致预测错误。
AI.method: 提出疑似对象转换机制（SOT），动态识别激活图中的疑似对象，并结合关键词感知判别模块（KAD）和随机连接探索策略（ERC）优化预测。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法有效提升了单阶段视觉定位模型的准确性，尤其在复杂场景下表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 通过聚焦疑似对象的关系建模与判别优化，本文为单阶段视觉定位提供了新的改进方向。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种针对单阶段视觉定位模型的改进方法，通过处理可能干扰模型预测的‘疑似对象’来提升性能。

authors_text: Ruifei He,Shuyang Sun,Jihan Yang,Song Bai,Xiaojuan Qi
title: Knowledge Distillation as Efficient Pre-training: Faster Convergence, Higher Data-efficiency, and Better Transferability
title_zh: 知识蒸馏作为高效的预训练：更快的收敛、更高的数据效率和更好的可迁移性
abstract: Large-scale pre-training has been proven to be crucial for various computer vision tasks. However, with the increase of pre-training data amount, model architecture amount, and the private/inaccessible data, it is not very efficient or possible to pre-train all the model architectures on large-scale datasets. In this work, we investigate an alternative strategy for pre-training, namely Knowledge Distillation as Efficient Pre-training (KDEP), aiming to efficiently transfer the learned feature representation from existing pre-trained models to new student models for future downstream tasks. We observe that existing Knowledge Distillation (KD) methods are unsuitable towards pre-training since they normally distill the logits that are going to be discarded when transferred to downstream tasks. To resolve this problem, we propose a feature-based KD method with non-parametric feature dimension aligning. Notably, our method performs comparably with supervised pre-training counterparts in 3 downstream tasks and 9 downstream datasets requiring 10x less data and 5x less pre-training time. Code is available at https://github.com/CVMI-Lab/KDEP.
summary: Large-scale pre-training has been proven to be crucial for various computer vision tasks. However, with the increase of pre-training data amount, model architecture amount, and the private/inaccessible data, it is not very efficient or possible to pre-train all the model architectures on large-scale datasets. In this work, we investigate an alternative strategy for pre-training, namely Knowledge Distillation as Efficient Pre-training (KDEP), aiming to efficiently transfer the learned feature representation from existing pre-trained models to new student models for future downstream tasks. We observe that existing Knowledge Distillation (KD) methods are unsuitable towards pre-training since they normally distill the logits that are going to be discarded when transferred to downstream tasks. To resolve this problem, we propose a feature-based KD method with non-parametric feature dimension aligning. Notably, our method performs comparably with supervised pre-training counterparts in 3 downstream tasks and 9 downstream datasets requiring 10x less data and 5x less pre-training time. Code is available at https://github.com/CVMI-Lab/KDEP.
abstract_zh: 大规模预训练已被证明对于各种计算机视觉任务至关重要。然而，随着预训练数据量、模型架构数量以及私有/不可访问数据的增加，在大规模数据集上预训练所有模型架构并不是非常有效或不可能。在这项工作中，我们研究了一种预训练的替代策略，即知识蒸馏作为高效预训练（KDEP），旨在有效地将学习到的特征表示从现有的预训练模型转移到新的学生模型以用于未来的下游任务。我们观察到现有的知识蒸馏（KD）方法不适合预训练，因为它们通常会提取在转移到下游任务时将被丢弃的逻辑。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于特征的非参数特征维度对齐的 KD 方法。值得注意的是，我们的方法在 3 个下游任务和 9 个下游数据集上的性能与有监督的预训练方法相当，需要的数据量减少了 10 倍，预训练时间减少了 5 倍。代码可在 https://github.com/CVMI-Lab/KDEP 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为KDEP的知识蒸馏方法，旨在通过高效预训练将现有预训练模型的特征表示转移到新模型中，以提升下游任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 大规模预训练在计算机视觉任务中至关重要，但随着数据量、模型架构的增加以及私有数据的不可访问性，传统预训练方法效率低下且难以覆盖所有模型架构。
AI.method: 提出基于特征的知识蒸馏方法，通过非参数化特征维度对齐，避免传统方法对下游任务无关的logits进行蒸馏。
AI.result: 在3个下游任务和9个数据集上，KDEP相比监督预训练方法仅需1/10的数据和1/5的预训练时间即可达到相近性能。
AI.conclusion: KDEP为高效预训练提供了可行替代方案，在减少数据和计算成本的同时保持模型性能，适用于资源受限场景。

search_text: Knowledge Distillation as Efficient Pre-training: Faster Convergence, Higher Data-efficiency, and Better Transferability 知识蒸馏作为高效的预训练：更快的收敛、更高的数据效率和更好的可迁移性 Large-scale pre-training has been proven to be crucial for various computer vision tasks. However, with the increase of pre-training data amount, model architecture amount, and the private/inaccessible data, it is not very efficient or possible to pre-train all the model architectures on large-scale datasets. In this work, we investigate an alternative strategy for pre-training, namely Knowledge Distillation as Efficient Pre-training (KDEP), aiming to efficiently transfer the learned feature representation from existing pre-trained models to new student models for future downstream tasks. We observe that existing Knowledge Distillation (KD) methods are unsuitable towards pre-training since they normally distill the logits that are going to be discarded when transferred to downstream tasks. To resolve this problem, we propose a feature-based KD method with non-parametric feature dimension aligning. Notably, our method performs comparably with supervised pre-training counterparts in 3 downstream tasks and 9 downstream datasets requiring 10x less data and 5x less pre-training time. Code is available at https://github.com/CVMI-Lab/KDEP. 大规模预训练已被证明对于各种计算机视觉任务至关重要。然而，随着预训练数据量、模型架构数量以及私有/不可访问数据的增加，在大规模数据集上预训练所有模型架构并不是非常有效或不可能。在这项工作中，我们研究了一种预训练的替代策略，即知识蒸馏作为高效预训练（KDEP），旨在有效地将学习到的特征表示从现有的预训练模型转移到新的学生模型以用于未来的下游任务。我们观察到现有的知识蒸馏（KD）方法不适合预训练，因为它们通常会提取在转移到下游任务时将被丢弃的逻辑。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于特征的非参数特征维度对齐的 KD 方法。值得注意的是，我们的方法在 3 个下游任务和 9 个下游数据集上的性能与有监督的预训练方法相当，需要的数据量减少了 10 倍，预训练时间减少了 5 倍。代码可在 https://github.com/CVMI-Lab/KDEP 获取。 本文提出了一种名为KDEP的知识蒸馏方法，旨在通过高效预训练将现有预训练模型的特征表示转移到新模型中，以提升下游任务的性能。 Ruifei He,Shuyang Sun,Jihan Yang,Song Bai,Xiaojuan Qi cs.CV

authors_text: Chang Liu,Chun Yang,Hai-Bo Qin,Xiaobin Zhu,Cheng-Lin Liu,Xu-Cheng Yin
title: Towards Open-Set Text Recognition via Label-to-Prototype Learning
title_zh: 通过标签到原型学习实现开放集文本识别
comment: V3 is a major revision of v2, should be close to the final form
comments: V3 is a major revision of v2, should be close to the final form
abstract: Scene text recognition is a popular topic and extensively used in the industry. Although many methods have achieved satisfactory performance for the close-set text recognition challenges, these methods lose feasibility in open-set scenarios, where collecting data or retraining models for novel characters could yield a high cost. For example, annotating samples for foreign languages can be expensive, whereas retraining the model each time when a novel character is discovered from historical documents costs both time and resources. In this paper, we introduce and formulate a new open-set text recognition task which demands the capability to spot and recognize novel characters without retraining. A label-to-prototype learning framework is also proposed as a baseline for the proposed task. Specifically, the framework introduces a generalizable label-to-prototype mapping function to build prototypes (class centers) for both seen and unseen classes. An open-set predictor is then utilized to recognize or reject samples according to the prototypes. The implementation of rejection capability over out-of-set characters allows automatic spotting of unknown characters in the incoming data stream. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves promising performance on a variety of zero-shot, close-set, and open-set text recognition datasets
summary: Scene text recognition is a popular topic and extensively used in the industry. Although many methods have achieved satisfactory performance for the close-set text recognition challenges, these methods lose feasibility in open-set scenarios, where collecting data or retraining models for novel characters could yield a high cost. For example, annotating samples for foreign languages can be expensive, whereas retraining the model each time when a novel character is discovered from historical documents costs both time and resources. In this paper, we introduce and formulate a new open-set text recognition task which demands the capability to spot and recognize novel characters without retraining. A label-to-prototype learning framework is also proposed as a baseline for the proposed task. Specifically, the framework introduces a generalizable label-to-prototype mapping function to build prototypes (class centers) for both seen and unseen classes. An open-set predictor is then utilized to recognize or reject samples according to the prototypes. The implementation of rejection capability over out-of-set characters allows automatic spotting of unknown characters in the incoming data stream. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves promising performance on a variety of zero-shot, close-set, and open-set text recognition datasets
abstract_zh: 场景文本识别是一个热门话题，在业界得到广泛应用。尽管许多方法在近距离文本识别挑战中取得了令人满意的性能，但这些方法在开放场景中失去了可行性，在开放场景中，收集新字符的数据或重新训练模型可能会产生很高的成本。例如，为外语样本进行注释可能会很昂贵，而每次从历史文档中发现新字符时重新训练模型既需要时间又需要资源。在本文中，我们介绍并制定了一种新的开放集文本识别任务，该任务需要能够在无需重新训练的情况下发现和识别新字符的能力。还提出了从标签到原型的学习框架作为所提议任务的基线。具体来说，该框架引入了一个可通用的标签到原型映射函数来为可见类和未见类构建原型（类中心）。然后使用开集预测器根据原型识别或拒绝样本。对超出设置的字符的拒绝能力的实现允许自动发现传入数据流中的未知字符。大量实验表明，我们的方法在各种零样本、闭集和开放集文本识别数据集上取得了良好的性能

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的开放集文本识别任务，并引入了一种标签到原型的学习框架，以在无需重新训练的情况下识别新字符。
AI.motivation: 尽管许多方法在封闭集文本识别任务中表现良好，但它们在开放集场景中不可行，因为收集数据或重新训练模型以处理新字符的成本很高。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种标签到原型的学习框架，引入了一种可推广的标签到原型映射函数，为已见和未见类别构建原型（类别中心），并使用开放集预测器根据原型识别或拒绝样本。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多种零样本、封闭集和开放集文本识别数据集上均取得了令人满意的性能。
AI.conclusion: 本文的方法为开放集文本识别任务提供了一个有效的解决方案，能够在不重新训练模型的情况下自动识别未知字符。

authors_text: Ganglai Wang,Peng Zhang,Lei Xie,Wei Huang,Yufei Zha,Yanning Zhang
title: An Audio-Visual Attention Based Multimodal Network for Fake Talking Face Videos Detection
title_zh: 基于视听注意力的多模态网络，用于假说话人脸视频检测
abstract: DeepFake based digital facial forgery is threatening the public media security, especially when lip manipulation has been used in talking face generation, the difficulty of fake video detection is further improved. By only changing lip shape to match the given speech, the facial features of identity is hard to be discriminated in such fake talking face videos. Together with the lack of attention on audio stream as the prior knowledge, the detection failure of fake talking face generation also becomes inevitable. Inspired by the decision-making mechanism of human multisensory perception system, which enables the auditory information to enhance post-sensory visual evidence for informed decisions output, in this study, a fake talking face detection framework FTFDNet is proposed by incorporating audio and visual representation to achieve more accurate fake talking face videos detection. Furthermore, an audio-visual attention mechanism (AVAM) is proposed to discover more informative features, which can be seamlessly integrated into any audio-visual CNN architectures by modularization. With the additional AVAM, the proposed FTFDNet is able to achieve a better detection performance on the established dataset (FTFDD). The evaluation of the proposed work has shown an excellent performance on the detection of fake talking face videos, which is able to arrive at a detection rate above 97%.
summary: DeepFake based digital facial forgery is threatening the public media security, especially when lip manipulation has been used in talking face generation, the difficulty of fake video detection is further improved. By only changing lip shape to match the given speech, the facial features of identity is hard to be discriminated in such fake talking face videos. Together with the lack of attention on audio stream as the prior knowledge, the detection failure of fake talking face generation also becomes inevitable. Inspired by the decision-making mechanism of human multisensory perception system, which enables the auditory information to enhance post-sensory visual evidence for informed decisions output, in this study, a fake talking face detection framework FTFDNet is proposed by incorporating audio and visual representation to achieve more accurate fake talking face videos detection. Furthermore, an audio-visual attention mechanism (AVAM) is proposed to discover more informative features, which can be seamlessly integrated into any audio-visual CNN architectures by modularization. With the additional AVAM, the proposed FTFDNet is able to achieve a better detection performance on the established dataset (FTFDD). The evaluation of the proposed work has shown an excellent performance on the detection of fake talking face videos, which is able to arrive at a detection rate above 97%.
abstract_zh: 基于 DeepFake 的数字面部伪造正在威胁公共媒体安全，特别是当嘴唇操纵被用于说话面部生成时，伪造视频检测的难度进一步提高。通过仅改变嘴唇形状以匹配给定的语音，在这种假说话面部视频中很难区分身份的面部特征。再加上缺乏对音频流作为先验知识的关注，假说话面孔生成的检测失败也变得不可避免。受人类多感官感知系统的决策机制的启发，该机制使听觉信息能够增强后感官视觉证据以提供明智的决策输出，本研究提出了一种假说话人脸检测框架FTFDNet，通过结合音频和视觉表示来实现更准确的假说话人脸视频检测。此外，提出了一种视听注意力机制（AVAM）来发现更多信息特征，可以通过模块化无缝集成到任何视听 CNN 架构中。通过额外的 AVAM，所提出的 FTFDNet 能够在已建立的数据集（FTFDD）上实现更好的检测性能。所提出的工作的评估在假说话人脸视频的检测上表现出了出色的性能，能够达到 97% 以上的检测率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于音频-视觉注意力的多模态网络FTFDNet，用于检测伪造的说话人脸视频。
AI.motivation: 深度伪造技术通过操纵嘴唇形状生成虚假说话视频，导致身份特征难以区分，且现有方法未充分结合音频线索，检测效果受限。
AI.method: 设计融合音频和视觉信息的FTFDNet框架，并引入模块化的音频-视觉注意力机制（AVAM）以提取更有效的跨模态特征。
AI.result: 在FTFDD数据集上实现超过97%的检测准确率，显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过结合音频线索与视觉注意力机制，该方法有效提升了伪造说话视频的检测性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于音频-视觉注意力的多模态网络FTFDNet，用于检测伪造的说话人脸视频。
ai_content.motivation: 深度伪造技术通过操纵嘴唇形状生成虚假说话视频，导致身份特征难以区分，且现有方法未充分结合音频线索，检测效果受限。
ai_content.method: 设计融合音频和视觉信息的FTFDNet框架，并引入模块化的音频-视觉注意力机制（AVAM）以提取更有效的跨模态特征。
ai_content.result: 在FTFDD数据集上实现超过97%的检测准确率，显著优于现有方法。
ai_content.conclusion: 通过结合音频线索与视觉注意力机制，该方法有效提升了伪造说话视频的检测性能。

search_text: An Audio-Visual Attention Based Multimodal Network for Fake Talking Face Videos Detection 基于视听注意力的多模态网络，用于假说话人脸视频检测 DeepFake based digital facial forgery is threatening the public media security, especially when lip manipulation has been used in talking face generation, the difficulty of fake video detection is further improved. By only changing lip shape to match the given speech, the facial features of identity is hard to be discriminated in such fake talking face videos. Together with the lack of attention on audio stream as the prior knowledge, the detection failure of fake talking face generation also becomes inevitable. Inspired by the decision-making mechanism of human multisensory perception system, which enables the auditory information to enhance post-sensory visual evidence for informed decisions output, in this study, a fake talking face detection framework FTFDNet is proposed by incorporating audio and visual representation to achieve more accurate fake talking face videos detection. Furthermore, an audio-visual attention mechanism (AVAM) is proposed to discover more informative features, which can be seamlessly integrated into any audio-visual CNN architectures by modularization. With the additional AVAM, the proposed FTFDNet is able to achieve a better detection performance on the established dataset (FTFDD). The evaluation of the proposed work has shown an excellent performance on the detection of fake talking face videos, which is able to arrive at a detection rate above 97%. 基于 DeepFake 的数字面部伪造正在威胁公共媒体安全，特别是当嘴唇操纵被用于说话面部生成时，伪造视频检测的难度进一步提高。通过仅改变嘴唇形状以匹配给定的语音，在这种假说话面部视频中很难区分身份的面部特征。再加上缺乏对音频流作为先验知识的关注，假说话面孔生成的检测失败也变得不可避免。受人类多感官感知系统的决策机制的启发，该机制使听觉信息能够增强后感官视觉证据以提供明智的决策输出，本研究提出了一种假说话人脸检测框架FTFDNet，通过结合音频和视觉表示来实现更准确的假说话人脸视频检测。此外，提出了一种视听注意力机制（AVAM）来发现更多信息特征，可以通过模块化无缝集成到任何视听 CNN 架构中。通过额外的 AVAM，所提出的 FTFDNet 能够在已建立的数据集（FTFDD）上实现更好的检测性能。所提出的工作的评估在假说话人脸视频的检测上表现出了出色的性能，能够达到 97% 以上的检测率。 本文提出一种基于音频-视觉注意力的多模态网络FTFDNet，用于检测伪造的说话人脸视频。 Ganglai Wang,Peng Zhang,Lei Xie,Wei Huang,Yufei Zha,Yanning Zhang cs.CV,cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Longhui Wei,Lingxi Xie,Wengang Zhou,Houqiang Li,Qi Tian
title: MVP: Multimodality-guided Visual Pre-training
title_zh: MVP：多模态引导的视觉预训练
abstract: Recently, masked image modeling (MIM) has become a promising direction for visual pre-training. In the context of vision transformers, MIM learns effective visual representation by aligning the token-level features with a pre-defined space (e.g., BEIT used a d-VAE trained on a large image corpus as the tokenizer). In this paper, we go one step further by introducing guidance from other modalities and validating that such additional knowledge leads to impressive gains for visual pre-training. The proposed approach is named Multimodality-guided Visual Pre-training (MVP), in which we replace the tokenizer with the vision branch of CLIP, a vision-language model pre-trained on 400 million image-text pairs. We demonstrate the effectiveness of MVP by performing standard experiments, i.e., pre-training the ViT models on ImageNet and fine-tuning them on a series of downstream visual recognition tasks. In particular, pre-training ViT-Base/16 for 300 epochs, MVP reports a 52.4% mIoU on ADE20K, surpassing BEIT (the baseline and previous state-of-the-art) with an impressive margin of 6.8%.
summary: Recently, masked image modeling (MIM) has become a promising direction for visual pre-training. In the context of vision transformers, MIM learns effective visual representation by aligning the token-level features with a pre-defined space (e.g., BEIT used a d-VAE trained on a large image corpus as the tokenizer). In this paper, we go one step further by introducing guidance from other modalities and validating that such additional knowledge leads to impressive gains for visual pre-training. The proposed approach is named Multimodality-guided Visual Pre-training (MVP), in which we replace the tokenizer with the vision branch of CLIP, a vision-language model pre-trained on 400 million image-text pairs. We demonstrate the effectiveness of MVP by performing standard experiments, i.e., pre-training the ViT models on ImageNet and fine-tuning them on a series of downstream visual recognition tasks. In particular, pre-training ViT-Base/16 for 300 epochs, MVP reports a 52.4% mIoU on ADE20K, surpassing BEIT (the baseline and previous state-of-the-art) with an impressive margin of 6.8%.
abstract_zh: 近年来，掩模图像建模（MIM）已成为视觉预训练的一个有前途的方向。在视觉转换器的背景下，MIM 通过将标记级特征与预定义空间对齐来学习有效的视觉表示（例如，BEIT 使用在大型图像语料库上训练的 d-VAE 作为标记器）。在本文中，我们更进一步，引入其他模式的指导，并验证这些额外的知识可以为视觉预训练带来令人印象深刻的收益。所提出的方法被称为多模态引导视觉预训练（MVP），其中我们用 CLIP 的视觉分支替换分词器，CLIP 是一种在 4 亿个图像文本对上预训练的视觉语言模型。我们通过执行标准实验来证明 MVP 的有效性，即在 ImageNet 上预训练 ViT 模型并在一系列下游视觉识别任务上对其进行微调。特别是，对 ViT-Base/16 进行 300 个 epoch 的预训练后，MVP 在 ADE20K 上的 mIoU 达到了 52.4%，超过了 BEIT（基线和之前的最先进水平），达到了令人印象深刻的 6.8%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多模态引导的视觉预训练方法MVP，通过引入CLIP模型的视觉分支作为分词器，提升视觉表征能力。
AI.motivation: 现有掩码图像建模（MIM）方法依赖单一视觉模态，缺乏跨模态信息引导，难以充分学习语义丰富的视觉表征。
AI.method: 将CLIP模型的视觉分支作为分词器替代传统d-VAE，在视觉预训练中引入文本模态的先验知识，通过跨模态对齐优化视觉特征学习。
AI.result: 在ADE20K数据集上，MVP预训练的ViT-Base/16模型达到52.4% mIoU，较BEIT基线提升6.8%，在多个下游视觉任务中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 多模态引导的视觉预训练能有效提升模型表征能力，MVP方法验证了跨模态信息对视觉预训练的显著促进作用。

In this paper, we go one step further by introducing guidance from other modalities and validating that such additional knowledge leads to impressive gains for visual pre-training. The proposed approach is named Multimodality-guided Visual Pre-training (MVP), in which we replace the tokenizer with the vision branch of CLIP, a vision-language model pre-trained on 400 million image-text pairs. authors_text: Tony Y. Sun,Shreyas Bhave,Jaan Altosaar,Noémie Elhadad
title: Assessing Phenotype Definitions for Algorithmic Fairness
title_zh: 评估表型定义的算法公平性
comment: American Medical Informatics Association (AMIA) 2022 - Accepted paper and presentation Conference on Health, Inference, and Learning (CHIL) 2022 - Invited non-archival presentation
comments: American Medical Informatics Association (AMIA) 2022 - Accepted paper and presentation Conference on Health, Inference, and Learning (CHIL) 2022 - Invited non-archival presentation
abstract: Disease identification is a core, routine activity in observational health research. Cohorts impact downstream analyses, such as how a condition is characterized, how patient risk is defined, and what treatments are studied. It is thus critical to ensure that selected cohorts are representative of all patients, independently of their demographics or social determinants of health. While there are multiple potential sources of bias when constructing phenotype definitions which may affect their fairness, it is not standard in the field of phenotyping to consider the impact of different definitions across subgroups of patients. In this paper, we propose a set of best practices to assess the fairness of phenotype definitions. We leverage established fairness metrics commonly used in predictive models and relate them to commonly used epidemiological cohort description metrics. We describe an empirical study for Crohn's disease and diabetes type 2, each with multiple phenotype definitions taken from the literature across two sets of patient subgroups (gender and race). We show that the different phenotype definitions exhibit widely varying and disparate performance according to the different fairness metrics and subgroups. We hope that the proposed best practices can help in constructing fair and inclusive phenotype definitions.
summary: Disease identification is a core, routine activity in observational health research. Cohorts impact downstream analyses, such as how a condition is characterized, how patient risk is defined, and what treatments are studied. It is thus critical to ensure that selected cohorts are representative of all patients, independently of their demographics or social determinants of health. While there are multiple potential sources of bias when constructing phenotype definitions which may affect their fairness, it is not standard in the field of phenotyping to consider the impact of different definitions across subgroups of patients. In this paper, we propose a set of best practices to assess the fairness of phenotype definitions. We leverage established fairness metrics commonly used in predictive models and relate them to commonly used epidemiological cohort description metrics. We describe an empirical study for Crohn's disease and diabetes type 2, each with multiple phenotype definitions taken from the literature across two sets of patient subgroups (gender and race). We show that the different phenotype definitions exhibit widely varying and disparate performance according to the different fairness metrics and subgroups. We hope that the proposed best practices can help in constructing fair and inclusive phenotype definitions.
abstract_zh: 疾病识别是观察健康研究的核心常规活动。队列影响下游分析，例如如何表征病情、如何定义患者风险以及研究哪些治疗方法。因此，确保选定的队列能够代表所有患者至关重要，无论其人口统计数据或健康的社会决定因素如何。虽然在构建表型定义时存在多种潜在的偏差来源，可能会影响其公平性，但在表型分析领域，考虑不同定义对患者亚组的影响并不是标准。在本文中，我们提出了一套评估表型定义公平性的最佳实践。我们利用预测模型中常用的既定公平性指标，并将其与常用的流行病学队列描述指标相关联。我们描述了一项针对克罗恩病和 2 型糖尿病的实证研究，每种研究都有多种表型定义，这些表型定义取自两组患者亚组（性别和种族）的文献。我们表明，根据不同的公平性指标和子组，不同的表型定义表现出广泛变化和不同的性能。我们希望所提出的最佳实践能够帮助构建公平和包容性的表型定义。

AI.tldr: 本文提出评估表型定义算法公平性的最佳实践，确保健康研究中患者队列的代表性，减少因人口统计学或社会健康决定因素导致的偏见。
AI.motivation: 现有表型定义在构建时未充分考虑不同患者子群体（如性别、种族）间的公平性，可能导致下游分析结果偏差，影响疾病特征定义和治疗研究的准确性。
AI.method: 结合预测模型的公平性指标与流行病学队列描述指标，通过克罗恩病和2型糖尿病的实证研究，分析多种表型定义在性别和种族子群体中的性能差异。
AI.result: 不同表型定义在公平性指标和子群体间的性能差异显著，部分定义在特定群体中表现不佳，凸显公平性评估的必要性。
AI.conclusion: 提出系统性评估方法，帮助构建更公平、包容的表型定义，提升健康研究的科学性和社会公正性。

method: 结合预测模型的公平性指标与流行病学队列描述指标，通过克罗恩病和2型糖尿病的实证研究，分析多种表型定义在性别和种族子群体中的性能差异。
result: 不同表型定义在公平性指标和子群体间的性能差异显著，部分定义在特定群体中表现不佳，凸显公平性评估的必要性。
conclusion: 提出系统性评估方法，帮助构建更公平、包容的表型定义，提升健康研究的科学性和社会公正性。
search_text: Assessing Phenotype Definitions for Algorithmic Fairness 评估表型定义的算法公平性 Disease identification is a core, routine activity in observational health research. Cohorts impact downstream analyses, such as how a condition is characterized, how patient risk is defined, and what treatments are studied. It is thus critical to ensure that selected cohorts are representative of all patients, independently of their demographics or social determinants of health. While there are multiple potential sources of bias when constructing phenotype definitions which may affect their fairness, it is not standard in the field of phenotyping to consider the impact of different definitions across subgroups of patients. In this paper, we propose a set of best practices to assess the fairness of phenotype definitions. We leverage established fairness metrics commonly used in predictive models and relate them to commonly used epidemiological cohort description metrics. We describe an empirical study for Crohn's disease and diabetes type 2, each with multiple phenotype definitions taken from the literature across two sets of patient subgroups (gender and race). We show that the different phenotype definitions exhibit widely varying and disparate performance according to the different fairness metrics and subgroups. We hope that the proposed best practices can help in constructing fair and inclusive phenotype definitions. 疾病识别是观察健康研究的核心常规活动。队列影响下游分析，例如如何表征病情、如何定义患者风险以及研究哪些治疗方法。因此，确保选定的队列能够代表所有患者至关重要，无论其人口统计数据或健康的社会决定因素如何。虽然在构建表型定义时存在多种潜在的偏差来源，可能会影响其公平性，但在表型分析领域，考虑不同定义对患者亚组的影响并不是标准。在本文中，我们提出了一套评估表型定义公平性的最佳实践。我们利用预测模型中常用的既定公平性指标，并将其与常用的流行病学队列描述指标相关联。我们描述了一项针对克罗恩病和 2 型糖尿病的实证研究，每种研究都有多种表型定义，这些表型定义取自两组患者亚组（性别和种族）的文献。我们表明，根据不同的公平性指标和子组，不同的表型定义表现出广泛变化和不同的性能。我们希望所提出的最佳实践能够帮助构建公平和包容性的表型定义。 本文提出评估表型定义算法公平性的最佳实践，确保健康研究中患者队列的代表性，减少因人口统计学或社会健康决定因素导致的偏见。 Tony Y. Sun,Shreyas Bhave,Jaan Altosaar,Noémie Elhadad q-bio.OT,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Sanskar Soni,Satyendra Singh Chouhan,Santosh Singh Rathore
title: TextConvoNet:A Convolutional Neural Network based Architecture for Text Classification
title_zh: TextConvoNet：基于卷积神经网络的文本分类架构
abstract: In recent years, deep learning-based models have significantly improved the Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. Specifically, the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), initially used for computer vision, has shown remarkable performance for text data in various NLP problems. Most of the existing CNN-based models use 1-dimensional convolving filters n-gram detectors), where each filter specialises in extracting n-grams features of a particular input word embedding. The input word embeddings, also called sentence matrix, is treated as a matrix where each row is a word vector. Thus, it allows the model to apply one-dimensional convolution and only extract n-gram based features from a sentence matrix. These features can be termed as intra-sentence n-gram features. To the extent of our knowledge, all the existing CNN models are based on the aforementioned concept. In this paper, we present a CNN-based architecture TextConvoNet that not only extracts the intra-sentence n-gram features but also captures the inter-sentence n-gram features in input text data. It uses an alternative approach for input matrix representation and applies a two-dimensional multi-scale convolutional operation on the input. To evaluate the performance of TextConvoNet, we perform an experimental study on five text classification datasets. The results are evaluated by using various performance metrics. The experimental results show that the presented TextConvoNet outperforms state-of-the-art machine learning and deep learning models for text classification purposes.
summary: In recent years, deep learning-based models have significantly improved the Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. Specifically, the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), initially used for computer vision, has shown remarkable performance for text data in various NLP problems. Most of the existing CNN-based models use 1-dimensional convolving filters n-gram detectors), where each filter specialises in extracting n-grams features of a particular input word embedding. The input word embeddings, also called sentence matrix, is treated as a matrix where each row is a word vector. Thus, it allows the model to apply one-dimensional convolution and only extract n-gram based features from a sentence matrix. These features can be termed as intra-sentence n-gram features. To the extent of our knowledge, all the existing CNN models are based on the aforementioned concept. In this paper, we present a CNN-based architecture TextConvoNet that not only extracts the intra-sentence n-gram features but also captures the inter-sentence n-gram features in input text data. It uses an alternative approach for input matrix representation and applies a two-dimensional multi-scale convolutional operation on the input. To evaluate the performance of TextConvoNet, we perform an experimental study on five text classification datasets. The results are evaluated by using various performance metrics. The experimental results show that the presented TextConvoNet outperforms state-of-the-art machine learning and deep learning models for text classification purposes.
abstract_zh: 近年来，基于深度学习的模型显着改善了自然语言处理（NLP）任务。具体来说，最初用于计算机视觉的卷积神经网络（CNN）在各种 NLP 问题中针对文本数据表现出了卓越的性能。大多数现有的基于 CNN 的模型都使用一维卷积滤波器（n-gram 检测器），其中每个滤波器专门提取特定输入词嵌入的 n-gram 特征。输入词嵌入，也称为句子矩阵，被视为一个矩阵，其中每行都是一个词向量。因此，它允许模型应用一维卷积并且仅从句子矩阵中提取基于 n-gram 的特征。这些特征可以称为句子内 n-gram 特征。据我们所知，所有现有的 CNN 模型都是基于上述概念。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 CNN 的架构 TextConvoNet，它不仅可以提取句子内的 n-gram 特征，还可以捕获输入文本数据中的句子间 n-gram 特征。它使用另一种方法来表示输入矩阵，并对输入应用二维多尺度卷积运算。为了评估 TextConvoNet 的性能，我们对五个文本分类数据集进行了实验研究。通过使用各种性能指标来评估结果。实验结果表明，所提出的 TextConvoNet 在文本分类方面优于最先进的机器学习和深度学习模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的文本分类架构TextConvoNet，通过提取句子内和句子间的n-gram特征来提升分类性能。
AI.motivation: 现有CNN模型仅能提取句子内的n-gram特征，无法捕捉句子间的语义关联，限制了文本分类效果的进一步提升。
AI.method: 采用二维多尺度卷积操作，通过替代的输入矩阵表示方法同时提取句子内和句子间的n-gram特征。
AI.result: 在五个文本分类数据集上的实验表明，TextConvoNet优于现有的机器学习和深度学习模型。
AI.conclusion: TextConvoNet通过同时建模句子内与句子间特征，有效提升了文本分类任务的性能。

authors_text: Devansh Saxena,Erina Seh-Young Moon,Dahlia Shehata,Shion Guha
title: Unpacking Invisible Work Practices, Constraints, and Latent Power Relationships in Child Welfare through Casenote Analysis
title_zh: 通过案例分析揭示儿童福利中的隐形工作实践、限制和潜在权力关系
abstract: Caseworkers are trained to write detailed narratives about families in Child-Welfare (CW) which informs collaborative high-stakes decision-making. Unlike other administrative data, these narratives offer a more credible source of information with respect to workers' interactions with families as well as underscore the role of systemic factors in decision-making. SIGCHI researchers have emphasized the need to understand human discretion at the street-level to be able to design human-centered algorithms for the public sector. In this study, we conducted computational text analysis of casenotes at a child-welfare agency in the midwestern United States and highlight patterns of invisible street-level discretionary work and latent power structures that have direct implications for algorithm design. Casenotes offer a unique lens for policymakers and CW leadership towards understanding the experiences of on-the-ground caseworkers. As a result of this study, we highlight how street-level discretionary work needs to be supported by sociotechnical systems developed through worker-centered design. This study offers the first computational inspection of casenotes and introduces them to the SIGCHI community as a critical data source for studying complex sociotechnical systems.
summary: Caseworkers are trained to write detailed narratives about families in Child-Welfare (CW) which informs collaborative high-stakes decision-making. Unlike other administrative data, these narratives offer a more credible source of information with respect to workers' interactions with families as well as underscore the role of systemic factors in decision-making. SIGCHI researchers have emphasized the need to understand human discretion at the street-level to be able to design human-centered algorithms for the public sector. In this study, we conducted computational text analysis of casenotes at a child-welfare agency in the midwestern United States and highlight patterns of invisible street-level discretionary work and latent power structures that have direct implications for algorithm design. Casenotes offer a unique lens for policymakers and CW leadership towards understanding the experiences of on-the-ground caseworkers. As a result of this study, we highlight how street-level discretionary work needs to be supported by sociotechnical systems developed through worker-centered design. This study offers the first computational inspection of casenotes and introduces them to the SIGCHI community as a critical data source for studying complex sociotechnical systems.
abstract_zh: 个案工作者接受过培训，能够撰写有关儿童福利 (CW) 家庭的详细叙述，为高风险的协作决策提供信息。与其他行政数据不同，这些叙述提供了有关工人与家庭互动的更可靠的信息来源，并强调了系统性因素在决策中的作用。 SIGCHI 研究人员强调，需要了解人类在街道层面的自由裁量权，以便能够为公共部门设计以人为本的算法。在这项研究中，我们对美国中西部一家儿童福利机构的案例说明进行了计算文本分析，并强调了对算法设计有直接影响的无形街道层面可自由支配工作的模式和潜在的权力结构。案例说明为政策制定者和 CW 领导层了解实地案例工作者的经历提供了独特的视角。这项研究的结果是，我们强调街道层面的自主工作需要如何得到通过以工人为中心的设计开发的社会技术系统的支持。这项研究首次对案例笔记进行了计算检查，并将其引入 SIGCHI 社区，作为研究复杂社会技术系统的关键数据源。

AI.tldr: 通过分析儿童福利案例笔记，揭示基层工作人员的无形工作实践和潜在权力结构，为设计支持其工作的社会技术系统提供依据。
AI.motivation: 理解基层决策中的社会技术因素，改进公共部门算法设计，提升儿童福利系统的效率与公平性。
AI.method: 采用计算文本分析方法，对美国中西部儿童福利机构的案例笔记进行量化与质性分析。
AI.result: 识别出基层工作人员的无形决策模式及潜在权力结构，揭示其对算法设计的直接影响。
AI.conclusion: 案例笔记是研究复杂社会技术系统的关键数据源，需通过以工作者为中心的设计构建支持系统的算法。

authors_text: Keval Doshi,Yasin Yilmaz
title: End-to-End Semantic Video Transformer for Zero-Shot Action Recognition
title_zh: 用于零镜头动作识别的端到端语义视频转换器
abstract: While video action recognition has been an active area of research for several years, zero-shot action recognition has only recently started gaining traction. In this work, we propose a novel end-to-end trained transformer model which is capable of capturing long range spatiotemporal dependencies efficiently, contrary to existing approaches which use 3D-CNNs. Moreover, to address a common ambiguity in the existing works about classes that can be considered as previously unseen, we propose a new experimentation setup that satisfies the zero-shot learning premise for action recognition by avoiding overlap between the training and testing classes. The proposed approach significantly outperforms the state of the arts in zero-shot action recognition in terms of the the top-1 accuracy on UCF-101, HMDB-51 and ActivityNet datasets. The code and proposed experimentation setup are available in GitHub: https://github.com/Secure-and-Intelligent-Systems-Lab/SemanticVideoTransformer

abstract_zh: 虽然视频动作识别多年来一直是一个活跃的研究领域，但零镜头动作识别最近才开始受到关注。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的端到端训练的 Transformer 模型，它能够有效地捕获长范围的时空依赖性，这与使用 3D-CNN 的现有方法相反。此外，为了解决现有工作中关于类的常见模糊性，这些类可以被认为是以前未见过的，我们提出了一种新的实验设置，通过避免训练类和测试类之间的重叠来满足动作识别的零样本学习前提。就 UCF-101、HMDB-51 和 ActivityNet 数据集上的 top-1 精度而言，所提出的方法明显优于零样本动作识别中的现有技术。代码和建议的实验设置可在 GitHub 中找到：https://github.com/Secure-and-Intelligent-Systems-Lab/SemanticVideoTransformer

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种端到端训练的语义视频Transformer模型，用于解决零样本动作识别问题。
AI.motivation: 现有动作识别方法多依赖3D-CNN，难以有效捕捉长距离时空依赖关系，且零样本学习场景下训练与测试类别重叠问题未被明确解决。
AI.method: 设计基于Transformer的端到端架构以高效建模时空依赖关系，并提出无类别重叠的新实验设置以符合零样本学习前提。
AI.result: 在UCF-101、HMDB-51和ActivityNet数据集上，该方法在零样本动作识别的top-1准确率显著优于现有最佳方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提模型及实验设置有效提升了零样本动作识别性能，验证了Transformer架构在该任务中的优势及新实验范式的有效性。
